text: Planning a trip is always exciting. Last year, I spent months researching a journey to Vietnam. I wanted to experience new cultures, so I read about local customs and traditions. When I arrived, the food amazed me—fresh herbs, rich soups, and street stalls filled with delicious smells. However, language barriers were a challenge; I often used hand gestures to order food or ask for directions. Despite this, people were friendly and patient. Now, I treasure those memories: the bustling markets, the kindness of strangers, and the taste of authentic pho. Travel taught me that even small adventures can change how you see the world.
